title: Used Ford Focus Buying Guide: Risks & What to Check | AutoVerdict
description: Buying a used Ford Focus? What to check first — the Powershift automatic, EcoBoost wet belt, and the key questions to ask the seller before viewing.
intro: The Focus is a sharp-driving and affordable used hatchback, but a couple of well-known drivetrain points are worth checking carefully. Here is what to verify before you view one.

faq.q: Which Ford Focus automatic should I avoid?
faq.a: The dry dual-clutch 'Powershift' automatic fitted to many Mk3 Focus models (roughly 2011–2016) gained a reputation for shuddering and clutch problems. A manual, or a later torque-converter/8-speed automatic, avoids that concern. If buying a Powershift car, check its repair history carefully.

faq.q: Is the Ford Focus EcoBoost engine reliable?
faq.a: The 1.0 EcoBoost is a well-liked engine but several use a 'wet' timing belt that runs in oil and needs replacing on schedule; neglect can cause problems. Confirm the belt service interval and whether it has been done, and ask about any coolant or overheating history on earlier units.

faq.q: Manual or automatic Focus for buying used?
faq.a: For Mk3-era cars, a manual sidesteps the Powershift concerns entirely and is often the simpler used buy. If you want an automatic, prioritise later cars with a documented gearbox history.
